framework_selection.reasoning: Paper explicitly names Qiskit and IBM Quantum Experience. No neural network training occurs — scikit-learn suffices for K-Means, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression. No GPU required; quantum simulation is CPU-bound on Qiskit Aer statevector simulator.

files.description: Package init; exposes QuantumSMOTE top-level class
Classical data preprocessing for tabular datasets

classes.name: TelcoChurnPreprocessor
classes.responsibility: Load raw CSV, clean nulls, type-convert, label-encode, OHE, MinMax-scale, return X [N,F] and y [N]

classes.name: BasePreprocessor
classes.responsibility: Abstract base; allows swapping in other tabular datasets

files.description: Dataset loading utilities; Kaggle CSV download helper

classes.name: TelcoChurnDataset
classes.responsibility: Locate or download telco-customer-churn.csv, return raw DataFrame

files.description: K-Means clustering wrapper that segments dataset and returns centroids

classes.name: KMeansClusterer
classes.responsibility: Fit K-Means on full X, return cluster labels and centroids [K, F]
public_methods: get_cluster_data(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, cluster_idx: int) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

files.description: Classical encoding of two vectors into quantum state vectors phi and psi (Algorithm 3)

classes.name: StatePreparation
classes.responsibility: Encode minority data point and centroid into amplitude-embedded quantum states for compact swap test
public_methods: prepare(data_point1: np.ndarray, data_point2: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
classes.notes: normalize_array: scale so sum-of-squares == 1.0. prepare returns (phi [2], psi [2F]) per Equations 4-6 and Algorithm 3.

files.description: Compact swap test quantum circuit (Algorithm 4); computes swap_test_probability and angular_distance

classes.name: CompactSwapTest
classes.responsibility: Build and execute Qiskit circuit implementing compact swap test; return swap_test_probability and angular_distance
public_methods: run(psi: np.ndarray, phi: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[float, float]
classes.design_notes: SWAPPABLE: statevector vs shot-based mode controlled by config flag use_statevector (confidence 0.65). If use_statevector=True, derive p0/p1 analytically from statevector amplitudes. If False, use shots=N and count outcomes.

classes.name: AngleCalculator
classes.responsibility: Map angular_distance → rotation angle using split_factor and loop iteration
public_methods: compute(angular_distance: float, split_factor: int, loop_ctr: int) -> float
classes.design_notes: Three branches: ad > pi/2, ad < 0, else. angle_increment=0.0174533 * loop_ctr added after branch selection.

files.description: Quantum rotation circuit: initialize statevector with minority sample, apply RX gates, extract real(statevector) (Algorithm 6)

classes.name: QuantumRotator
classes.responsibility: Apply RX(angle) to all qubits of minority statevector; return real part as synthetic data point
classes.design_notes: SWAPPABLE: extract_synthetic has configurable strategy (first-F / reshape). target_dim=F=32. No entangling gates. n_qubits = ceil(log2(F)) + add where add=1 if F not divisible by n else 0.

classes.name: QuantumSMOTE
classes.responsibility: Outer loop over clusters; compute synthetic_loop_itr per cluster; call StatePrep → SwapTest → AngleCalc → Rotator for each minority sample; return syn_dataframe
public_methods: fit_resample(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, cluster_labels: np.ndarray, centroids: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
generate_for_cluster(minority_X: np.ndarray, centroid: np.ndarray, n_synthetic: int) -> np.ndarray

files.description: Classification metrics: accuracy, F1, PR-AUC, ROC-AUC, confusion matrix

classes.name: ClassificationMetrics
classes.responsibility: Compute and format all metrics from paper; produce comparison tables
public_methods: compute(y_true: np.ndarray, y_pred: np.ndarray, y_proba: np.ndarray) -> dict

files.description: Random Forest and Logistic Regression wrappers with standard train/eval interface

classes.name: ClassifierFactory
classes.responsibility: Instantiate RF or LR from config; train and evaluate
public_methods: train_evaluate(model, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test) -> dict

classes.name: Config
classes.responsibility: Load config.yaml; expose typed attributes; validate required fields

files.description: Scatter plots (minority vs synthetic), distribution histograms, confusion matrix heatmaps, ROC/PR curves

classes.name: Visualizer
public_methods: plot_scatter(X, y, syn_X, feature_names, title) -> None

tensor_flow.name: Full Quantum-SMOTE Pipeline
pseudocode: swap_prob scalar, angular_dist scalar = swap_test.run(psi1, phi1)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X_aug, y_aug)
classifier.train_evaluate(model, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test) → metrics dict
OUTPUT: metrics dict {accuracy, f1, pr_auc, roc_auc, confusion_matrix}

tensor_flow.name: CompactSwapTest Circuit Forward Pass
pseudocode: n_qubits = log2(len(psi)) + 1   (e.g. log2(64)+1 = 7 total qubits: 1 ancilla q1 + 6 data)
p0 = counts['0'] / shots; p1 = counts['1'] / shots

tensor_flow.name: QuantumRotator Forward Pass
pseudocode: INPUT: minority_sample [F=32] float64, angle scalar float64
n = floor(log2(F)); add = 1 if F % n != 0 else 0
n_qubits = n  (e.g. for F=32, n=5, circuit has 5 qubits)

clustering.comment: # K=3 explicitly stated; init/n_init are sklearn defaults, confidence=0.75

comments.split_factor: # ASSUMED: paper tests 2/5/10/100; 5 used as default, confidence=0.55
comments.use_statevector: # ASSUMED: if True use exact statevector probs; if False use shots. Confidence=0.65

rationale.qiskit: Quantum circuit construction and simulation (explicitly stated in paper)
rationale.scikit-learn: K-Means clustering, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, metrics
rationale.tqdm: Progress bars for quantum circuit loops

entrypoints.description: Full end-to-end experiment: preprocess → cluster → SMOTE → train → evaluate → report
Run only the QuantumSMOTE data generation step; save synthetic CSV
Produce all paper figures from saved results
Evaluate a saved augmented dataset with a classifier

docker_spec.notes: No CUDA base image needed — quantum simulation is CPU-only on Qiskit Aer statevector simulator

risk_assessment.description: The mapping from statevector [2^n] back to synthetic feature vector [F=32] is ambiguous (SIR confidence 0.55). loop_ctr is passed to create_syn_data but its role in reshaping is not fully described. An incorrect extraction strategy will silently produce wrong synthetic samples with no obvious error signal.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement two strategies behind a config flag: 'first_F' (take real(statevector[:F])) and 'reshape' (reshape statevector by loop_ctr strides). Log norm of synthetic vs original to detect degenerate outputs. Add a unit test comparing synthetic point norm to original.

risk_assessment.description: split_factor used in the main experiment (Section 4) is not specified (SIR confidence 0.55). This directly controls how far synthetic points deviate from source, affecting all downstream metrics.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Run a sensitivity sweep over split_factor in {2, 5, 10} and log results for all three alongside final table. Default to 5 as mid-range.

risk_assessment.description: Algorithm 4 pseudocode uses shot counts, but statevector simulation gives exact probabilities. Using shots introduces sampling variance; using statevector diverges from pseudocode. (SIR confidence 0.65)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Config flag use_statevector=True by default for reproducibility (exact, no noise). Provide shots fallback mode. Document clearly in README.

risk_assessment.description: Paper performs correlation-based feature selection but does not list retained features or threshold. Arriving at exactly 32 features may require trial-and-error. (SIR confidence 0.55)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose correlation_threshold as a config param. Log final feature names to results/feature_names.txt. Include EDA notebook step to visualize correlogram before feature selection.

risk_assessment.description: Train/test split ratio not stated (SIR confidence 0.60). Wrong ratio affects all reported accuracy/F1 numbers.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to 80/20 stratified. Add a CLI flag --test-size. Log exact split sizes to results log.

risk_assessment.description: Quantum circuit simulation is invoked once per minority sample per synthetic iteration. For a dataset with ~1800 minority samples and target_pct=50% requiring ~2x synthetic samples, this means ~3600 Qiskit circuit executions. Statevector simulation is fast (<1ms per circuit) but the Python loop overhead may be significant.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add tqdm progress bars. Consider batch vectorization if runtime exceeds 10 minutes. Log per-cluster timing.

risk_assessment.description: Dataset is publicly available on Kaggle but requires Kaggle API credentials for programmatic download.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Include manual download instructions in README. Provide data/README.md with direct CSV placement instructions. Optionally include kaggle.json setup guide.

risk_assessment.description: Qiskit 1.0+ broke many legacy APIs (execute(), get_statevector()). Paper's pseudocode uses pre-1.0 API. (SIR confidence 0.75)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Pin qiskit==0.45.3 (last 0.x series) and qiskit-aer==0.13.3 for compatibility with pseudocode. Document upgrade path to Qiskit 1.x in NOTES.md.

unit_tests: test_state_preparation: verify phi [2] and psi [2F] are unit vectors; verify construction matches Equations 4-6
test_swap_test: known identical vectors → swap_prob=0, angular_dist=0; orthogonal vectors → swap_prob=1
test_angle_calculator: verify three branches produce angles in expected ranges
test_rotator: RX(0) → statevector unchanged; verify output shape [F]
test_preprocessor: F=32 output shape; all values in [0,1]; target binary
integration_tests: test_full_pipeline_no_smote: run baseline RF and LR; check accuracy > 0.75
test_full_pipeline_30pct: run with target_pct=30; verify syn_dataframe row count
test_metrics_match_paper: run with seed=42; compare F1 and AUC to Table 1 within ±0.05 tolerance
